operator_request: Run evaluation tests on the just-implemented WP-FF-003 through different adversarial lenses, remediate findings, gaps, concerns, and risks, clean up code where needed, and use sub-agents to help.

basis.reason: This revision validates and hardens the accepted Phase 0 implementation without selecting a new architecture, dependency, public contract, or product feature.

preserve: All WP-FF-003 v1 acceptance requirements and proof limitations
Three-root ownership and Rust-only dependency boundary
Phase 0 bootstrap scope and later-phase blocks
change: Add independent adversarial proof beyond the original evidence
Remediate confirmed false-PASS, failure-path, robustness, and maintainability defects
Add focused regression tests and simplify safely
non_goals: New product features, shipped crates, dependencies, or broad readiness claims

attack_lenses: Contract, acceptance, policy-schema, rule-to-proof, and false-PASS alignment
Hostile repository, filesystem, path, Git, toolchain, process, and report states
Rust parsing, error propagation, determinism, filesystem traversal, process handling, test isolation, and code quality
minimum_controls: At least two independent checks not cited by v1 evidence
Focused regression tests followed by full post-cleanup gates
plausible_failures: An aggregate report says PASS after a required child never ran
A fixture fails before exercising its intended diagnostic
Unknown or malformed policy fields are accepted
A path escape or missing repository/tool state bypasses validation
Parallel or interrupted report writers lose or corrupt evidence
Duplicated gate logic drifts and one route becomes weaker

execution_decomposition: Activate the v2 revision and capture exact Git state
Run three independent read-only adversarial audits
Reproduce and classify every material finding
Remediate confirmed defects with focused regression proof
Perform reuse, deletion, dependency, and simplification review
Run the full acceptance/risk/scope matrix and canonical gates
Record residual uncertainty and close only through main-containment proof
